This is not really a museum in the traditional sense. This is basically a fun space for those folks who like to pose for amusing photos in front of artwork. It consists of several rooms filled with murals or paintings (floor to ceiling ) of different scenes. You are supposed to pose in front of each mural in a way that creates the illusion that you are a part of the artwork. Some of the artwork is more convincing than others at creating a 3d illusion. They do offer examples on how to pose in front of each painted scene. They also have some props that you can use to enhance your photos.  There are markers on the floor which indicate where the person who is taking the photo should stand. One can be quite creative with the poses. Selfies are not going to give good results here. I recommend bringing a tripod if you want all those in your group to be included in the photos. I noticed that it was less expensive to buy tickets online than in person.

Best experience in San Francisco.  An amazing art studio of 3D images around the walls, to interact and take photos in.  This was such a good laugh, and made some really great memories here.  Follow the instructions printed on the floor to get the best position to take the photos.  There are props alongside some of the artwork which really added to the fun!   Highly recommend visiting.
